tEn: $π_{0.5}$: a Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization
tZh: $π_{0.5}$：一个具有开放世界泛化能力的视觉-语言-动作模型
sEn: A new VLA model, π0.5, achieves broad real-world generalization by co-training on heterogeneous tasks and data sources, enabling long-horizon dexterous manipulation in unseen homes.
sZh: 新VLA模型π0.5通过异构任务和数据源的协同训练，实现了广泛的现实世界泛化，能在未见过的家庭环境中执行长时程灵巧操作。
absEn: In order for robots to be useful, they must perform practically relevant tasks in the real world, outside of the lab. While vision-language-action (VLA) models have demonstrated impressive results for end-to-end robot control, it remains an open question how far such models can generalize in the wild. We describe $π_{0.5}$, a new model based on $π_{0}$ that uses co-training on heterogeneous tasks to enable broad generalization. $π_{0.5}$\ uses data from multiple robots, high-level semantic prediction, web data, and other sources to enable broadly generalizable real-world robotic manipulation. Our system uses a combination of co-training and hybrid multi-modal examples that combine image observations, language commands, object detections, semantic subtask prediction, and low-level actions. Our experiments show that this kind of knowledge transfer is essential for effective generalization, and we demonstrate for the first time that an end-to-end learning-enabled robotic system can perform long-horizon and dexterous manipulation skills, such as cleaning a kitchen or bedroom, in entirely new homes.
absZh: 为了让机器人真正有用，它们必须在实验室外的现实世界中执行实际相关的任务。尽管视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在端到端机器人控制方面取得了令人印象深刻的结果，但这些模型在野外能泛化到何种程度仍是一个开放问题。我们描述了$π_{0.5}$，这是一个基于$π_{0}$的新模型，通过异构任务的协同训练实现了广泛的泛化。$π_{0.5}$利用来自多个机器人的数据、高层语义预测、网络数据和其他来源，实现了广泛泛化的现实世界机器人操作。我们的系统结合了协同训练和混合多模态示例，这些示例结合了图像观察、语言指令、物体检测、语义子任务预测和低级动作。我们的实验表明，这种知识迁移对于有效泛化至关重要，并且我们首次证明，一个端到端学习驱动的机器人系统可以在全新的家庭环境中执行长时程和灵巧的操作技能，例如清洁厨房或卧室。

contrib.en: Proposes π0.5, a VLA model co-trained on heterogeneous tasks for open-world generalization.
contrib.zh: 提出π0.5，一种通过异构任务协同训练实现开放世界泛化的视觉-语言-动作模型。

contrib.en: Demonstrates first end-to-end robotic system performing long-horizon dexterous tasks in unseen homes.
contrib.zh: 首次展示端到端机器人系统在未见过的家庭中执行长时灵巧操作任务。

contrib.en: Shows co-training with multi-robot data, web data, and high-level semantic prediction enables broad generalization.
contrib.zh: 表明与多机器人数据、网络数据和高层语义预测的协同训练可实现广泛泛化。

contrib.en: Introduces hierarchical inference: model predicts high-level subtask then low-level actions via flow matching.
contrib.zh: 引入分层推理：模型先预测高层子任务，再通过流匹配预测底层动作。

contrib.en: Validates that cross-embodiment transfer and web data are critical for generalization to new objects and scenes.
contrib.zh: 验证跨本体迁移和网络数据对泛化到新物体和场景至关重要。

contrib.en: Achieves comparable performance to model trained on test homes, bridging generalization gap via co-training.
contrib.zh: 达到与在测试家庭上训练的模型相当的性能，通过协同训练弥合泛化差距。

limits.en: Model still makes mistakes in unfamiliar environments like hard-to-open drawers or cabinets.
limits.zh: 模型在陌生环境中仍会犯错，如难以打开的抽屉或橱柜。

limits.en: Partial observability challenges, e.g., robot arm occluding spills during wiping tasks.
limits.zh: 部分可观测性挑战，例如机器人手臂在擦拭任务中遮挡污渍。

limits.en: High-level subtask inference can be distracted, e.g., repeatedly opening and closing a drawer.
limits.zh: 高层子任务推理易分心，例如反复开关抽屉。

limits.en: Limited context window and memory; cannot handle tasks requiring navigation between rooms or object memory.
limits.zh: 上下文窗口和记忆有限；无法处理需要跨房间导航或物体记忆的任务。

limits.en: Prompt complexity limited by training data; more intricate instructions require richer annotations.
limits.zh: 提示复杂度受限于训练数据；更复杂的指令需要更丰富的标注。

items.en: In order for robots to be useful, they must perform practically relevant tasks in the real world, outside of the lab. While vision-language-action (VLA) models have demonstrated impressive results for end-to-end robot control, it remains an open question how far such models can generalize in the wild. We describe π0.5\pi_{0.5}, a new model based on π0\pi_{0} that uses co-training on heterogeneous tasks to enable broad generalization. π0.5\pi_{0.5} uses data from multiple robots, high-level semantic prediction, web data, and other sources to enable broadly generalizable real-world robotic manipulation. Our system uses a combination of co-training and hybrid multi-modal examples that combine image observations, language commands, object detections, semantic subtask prediction, and low-level actions. Our experiments show that this kind of knowledge transfer is essential for effective generalization, and we demonstrate for the first time that an end-to-end learning-enabled robotic system can perform long-horizon and dexterous manipulation skills, such as cleaning a kitchen or bedroom, in entirely new homes.
items.zh: 为了使机器人有用，它们必须在现实世界中执行实际相关的任务，而不仅仅是在实验室中。尽管视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在端到端机器人控制方面展示了令人印象深刻的结果，但这些模型在现实环境中能泛化到何种程度仍是一个开放问题。我们描述了π0.5，一种基于π0的新模型，它利用异构任务的协同训练来实现广泛的泛化。π0.5使用来自多个机器人的数据、高层语义预测、网络数据和其他来源，以实现广泛可泛化的真实世界机器人操作。我们的系统结合了协同训练和混合多模态示例，这些示例结合了图像观测、语言指令、目标检测、语义子任务预测和底层动作。我们的实验表明，这种知识迁移对于有效泛化至关重要，并且我们首次证明，一个端到端学习驱动的机器人系统可以在全新的家庭中执行长时程和灵巧的操作技能，例如清洁厨房或卧室。

items.capEn: Figure 2: π0.5\pi_{0.5} cleaning a new kitchen. The robot is tasked with cleaning a kitchen in a home that was not in the training data. The model is given general tasks (close the cabinets, put the items in the drawer, wipe the spill, and put the dishes in the sink), which it performs by both predicting subtasks to accomplish (e.g., pick up the plate) and emitting low-level actions.
items.capZh: 图2：π0.5清洁一个新厨房。机器人被要求在一个训练数据中未出现的家庭厨房中进行清洁。模型被赋予一般性任务（关闭橱柜、将物品放入抽屉、擦拭溢出物、将盘子放入水槽），它通过预测要完成的子任务（例如，拿起盘子）并发出底层动作来执行这些任务。

items.en: Stuff your eyes with wonder… See the world. It’s more fantastic than any dream made or paid for in factories.
items.zh: 用惊奇填满你的眼睛……看看这个世界。它比任何在工厂中制造或付费的梦境都更加奇幻。

items.en: Open-world generalization represents one of the biggest open problems in physical intelligence: embodied systems such as robotic arms, humanoids, and autonomous vehicles only truly become useful when they can leave the lab and handle the diverse situations and unexpected events that occur in the real world. Learning-based systems offer a path to enabling broad generalization, particularly with recent advances that have enabled scalable learning systems in domains ranging from natural language processing [79, 21, 10, 78] to computer vision [34, 66, 35, 43]. However, the diversity of situations that a robot might encounter in the real world requires more than just scale: we need to design training recipes that can provide the breadth of knowledge that will allow robots to generalize at many levels of abstraction. For example, if a mobile robot is asked to clean up a kitchen that it has never seen before, some behaviors generalize readily if they are well represented in the data with a sufficient range of scenes and objects (e.g., picking up a knife or plate), others might require adapting or modifying existing skills to use them in a new way or in a new sequence, and yet others might require understanding the semantics of the scene based on prior knowledge (e.g., which drawer to open, or which object on the counter is most likely to be a drying rack). How can we structure a training recipe for a robotic learning system that can enable this kind of flexible generalization?
items.zh: 开放世界泛化是物理智能中最大的开放问题之一：具身系统，如机械臂、人形机器人和自动驾驶车辆，只有在能够离开实验室并处理现实世界中发生的各种情况和意外事件时，才真正变得有用。基于学习的系统为实现广泛泛化提供了一条路径，特别是在自然语言处理[79, 21, 10, 78]和计算机视觉[34, 66, 35, 43]等领域，最近的进展使得可扩展学习系统成为可能。然而，机器人在现实世界中可能遇到的情况的多样性不仅仅需要规模：我们需要设计训练方案，以提供能够使机器人在多个抽象层次上泛化的知识广度。例如，如果一个移动机器人被要求清理一个从未见过的厨房，一些行为如果数据中充分涵盖了各种场景和物体（例如，拿起刀或盘子），则容易泛化；其他行为可能需要调整或修改现有技能以新的方式或新的顺序使用；还有一些行为可能需要基于先验知识理解场景的语义（例如，打开哪个抽屉，或者台面上哪个物体最可能是晾碗架）。我们如何为机器人学习系统构建一个训练方案，使其能够实现这种灵活的泛化？

items.en: A person can draw on a lifetime of experience to synthesize appropriate solutions to each of these challenges. Not all of this experience is firsthand, and not all of it comes from rote practice – for example, we might use facts that we were told by others or read in a book, together with bits of insight from other tasks we have performed in different contexts, combined with direct experience in the target domain. Analogously, we might hypothesize that generalizable robotic learning systems must be able to transfer experience and knowledge from a variety of information sources. Some of these sources are firsthand experience with direct relevance to the task at hand, some require transfer from other robot embodiments, environments, or domains, and some represent entirely different data types, such as verbal instructions, perceptual tasks based on web data, or prediction of high-level semantic commands. The heterogeneity of these different sources of data present a major obstacle, but fortunately recent advances in vision-language-action (VLA) models provide us with a toolkit that can make this possible: by casting different modalities into the same sequence modeling framework, VLAs can be adapted to train on robot data, language data, computer vision tasks, and combinations of the above.
items.zh: 一个人可以凭借一生的经验来综合应对这些挑战中的每一个的适当解决方案。并非所有经验都是第一手的，也并非所有经验都来自机械练习——例如，我们可能会使用别人告诉我们的或从书中读到的事实，结合我们在不同背景下执行的其他任务的一些见解，再加上在目标领域的直接经验。类似地，我们可以假设，可泛化的机器人学习系统必须能够从各种信息源迁移经验和知识。其中一些来源是与手头任务直接相关的第一手经验，一些需要从其他机器人本体、环境或领域迁移，还有一些代表完全不同的数据类型，如口头指令、基于网络数据的感知任务，或高层语义命令的预测。这些不同数据源的异构性构成了一个主要障碍，但幸运的是，视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的最新进展为我们提供了使这成为可能的工具包：通过将不同模态转化为相同的序列建模框架，VLA可以适应于在机器人数据、语言数据、计算机视觉任务及其组合上进行训练。

items.en: In this paper, we leverage this observation to design a co-training framework for VLAs that can utilize heterogeneous and diverse knowledge sources to enable broad generalization. Building on the π0\pi_{0} VLA, we propose to include a range of different data sources to create the π0.5\pi_{0.5} model (“pi oh five”), which can control mobile manipulators to perform a variety of household tasks even in homes that were never seen during training. π0.5\pi_{0.5} draws on experience from many sources: in addition to a medium-sized dataset collected directly with mobile manipulators in a variety of real homes (about 400 hours), π0.5\pi_{0.5} uses data from other non-mobile robots, data of related tasks collected under laboratory conditions, training examples that require predicting “high-level” semantic tasks based on robot observation, verbal language instructions provided to the robot by human supervisors, and a variety of multi-modal examples created from web data, such as image captioning, question answering, and object localization (see Figure LABEL:fig:teaser). The overwhelming majority of training examples provided to π0.5\pi_{0.5} (97.6% during the first training phase) do not come from mobile manipulators performing household tasks, but from these other sources, such as other robots or data from the web. Nonetheless, π0.5\pi_{0.5} is able to control mobile manipulators in entirely new homes not seen during training, perform intricate tasks such as hanging up towels or making beds, and can carry out long-horizon manipulation skills 10 to 15 minutes in length, cleaning an entire kitchen or bedroom based on only a high-level prompt.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们利用这一观察结果设计了一个VLA的协同训练框架，该框架可以利用异构和多样化的知识源来实现广泛的泛化。基于π0 VLA，我们提出包含一系列不同的数据源来创建π0.5模型（“pi oh five”），该模型可以控制移动操作器执行各种家务任务，即使在训练期间从未见过的家庭中也是如此。π0.5从多个来源汲取经验：除了直接在多种真实家庭中使用移动操作器收集的中等规模数据集（约400小时）外，π0.5还使用来自其他非移动机器人的数据、在实验室条件下收集的相关任务数据、需要基于机器人观测预测“高层”语义任务的训练示例、人类监督员提供给机器人的口头语言指令，以及从网络数据创建的各种多模态示例，如图像描述、问答和目标定位（见图LABEL:fig:teaser）。提供给π0.5的绝大多数训练示例（第一阶段训练中占97.6%）并非来自执行家务任务的移动操作器，而是来自这些其他来源，如其他机器人或网络数据。尽管如此，π0.5能够在训练期间未见过的新家庭中控制移动操作器，执行诸如挂毛巾或铺床等复杂任务，并能执行长达10到15分钟的长时程操作技能，仅基于高层提示即可清洁整个厨房或卧室。

items.en: The design of π0.5\pi_{0.5} follows a simple hierarchical architecture: we first pre-train the model on the heterogeneous mixture of training tasks, and then fine-tune it specifically for mobile manipulation with both low-level action examples and high-level “semantic” actions, which correspond to predicting subtask labels such as “pick up the cutting board” or “rearrange the pillow.” At runtime, during each step of inference, the model first predicts the semantic subtask, inferring the behavior that is appropriate to perform next based on the task structure and the semantics of the scene, and then predicts the low-level robot action chunk based on this subtask. This simple architecture provides both the ability to reason about long-horizon multi-stage tasks and the ability to leverage different sources of knowledge for the two levels: the low-level action inference procedure readily benefits from action data collected by other robots, including simpler static robots in other environments, while the high-level inference procedure benefits from semantic examples from the web, high-level annotation prediction, and even verbal commands that can be provided to the robot by human “supervisors” that walk the robot through complex tasks step by step, instructing it (much like how they might instruct a person) on the appropriate subtasks to perform to complete a complex task such as cleaning a room. We illustrate this design in Figure LABEL:fig:teaser.
items.zh: π0.5的设计遵循一个简单的层次架构：我们首先在异构混合训练任务上预训练模型，然后专门针对移动操作进行微调，同时使用底层动作示例和高层“语义”动作，后者对应于预测子任务标签，如“拿起切菜板”或“重新摆放枕头”。在运行时，推理的每一步，模型首先预测语义子任务，根据任务结构和场景语义推断接下来要执行的适当行为，然后基于该子任务预测底层机器人动作块。这种简单的架构既提供了推理长时程多阶段任务的能力，也提供了利用不同层次知识源的能力：底层动作推理过程直接受益于其他机器人（包括其他环境中更简单的静态机器人）收集的动作数据，而高层推理过程受益于来自网络的语义示例、高层标注预测，甚至人类“监督员”提供给机器人的口头命令，这些监督员逐步引导机器人完成复杂任务（就像他们指导一个人一样），指示机器人执行完成复杂任务（如清洁房间）所需的适当子任务。我们在图LABEL:fig:teaser中展示了这一设计。

items.en: Our central contribution is a system for training a highly generalizable VLA, π0.5\pi_{0.5}, together with a proof of concept that generalization can emerge from this model when it is trained on appropriately diverse data. We provide a detailed empirical evaluation of both π0.5\pi_{0.5}’s generalization capabilities and the relevance of different co-training ingredients. To our knowledge, our work is the first to demonstrate an end-to-end learning-enabled robotic system that can perform long-horizon and dexterous manipulation skills, such as cleaning a kitchen or bedroom, in entirely new homes. Our experiments and comparisons further show that this is enabled by transferring knowledge from other robots, high-level semantic prediction, verbal language instruction from human supervisors, web data, and other sources.
items.zh: 我们的核心贡献是一个用于训练高度可泛化VLA π0.5的系统，以及一个概念验证，表明当该模型在适当多样化的数据上训练时，泛化能力可以从该模型中涌现。我们提供了对π0.5泛化能力以及不同协同训练成分相关性的详细实证评估。据我们所知，我们的工作是首次展示一个端到端学习驱动的机器人系统，可以在全新的家庭中执行长时程和灵巧的操作技能，例如清洁厨房或卧室。我们的实验和比较进一步表明，这是通过从其他机器人、高层语义预测、人类监督员的口头语言指令、网络数据和其他来源迁移知识而实现的。

items.en: Generalist robot manipulation policies. Recent works have demonstrated that broadening the training data distribution for robot manipulation policies from narrow, single-task datasets to diverse datasets that span many scenes and tasks [17, 25, 80, 63, 41, 6, 30, 67, 1] allows the resulting policies to not only solve a wider range of tasks out of the box, but also improves their ability to generalize to new scenes and tasks [9, 63, 62, 22]. Training such generalist policies requires new modeling approaches that can handle the scale and diversity of datasets that often span hundreds of different tasks and scenes. Vision-language-action models (VLAs) [23, 92, 42, 8, 83, 90, 55, 45, 3, 75, 64, 76, 84, 7, 37] offer an appealing solution: by fine-tuning pre-trained vision-language models for robot control, VLAs can leverage the semantic knowledge acquired from web-scale pretraining and bring it to bear on the robotics problem. When combined with highly expressive action decoding mechanisms like flow matching [8], diffusion [55, 84, 52], or advanced action tokenization schemes [64], VLAs can perform a wide range of complex manipulation tasks in the real world. However, despite impressive language following abilities, VLAs are still typically evaluated in environments that closely match their training data. While some studies suggest that simple skills like picking up objects or opening drawers can be made to generalize simply by collecting robot data in a broader set of environments [14, 67, 28, 49, 64], it is challenging to apply the same approach to more complex, long-horizon tasks like cleaning up a kitchen, where achieving broad coverage of plausible scenarios via brute-force scaling of robot data collection is infeasible. In our experiments, we evaluate π0.5\pi_{0.5} in entirely new scenes, such as new kitchens and bedrooms that were not seen in training, showing that our VLA can generalize to entirely new scenes by leveraging not only direct first-hand experience on the target mobile manipulator platform, but also information from other data sources. These sources include data from other (non-mobile) robots, high-level semantic subtask prediction, and data from the web.
items.zh: 通用机器人操作策略。最近的研究表明，将机器人操作策略的训练数据分布从狭窄的单任务数据集扩展到涵盖许多场景和任务的多样化数据集[17, 25, 80, 63, 41, 6, 30, 67, 1]，不仅使生成的策略能够直接解决更广泛的任务，而且提高了它们泛化到新场景和任务的能力[9, 63, 62, 22]。训练这样的通用策略需要新的建模方法，以处理通常涵盖数百个不同任务和场景的数据集的规模和多样性。视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）[23, 92, 42, 8, 83, 90, 55, 45, 3, 75, 64, 76, 84, 7, 37]提供了一种有吸引力的解决方案：通过将预训练的视觉-语言模型微调用于机器人控制，VLA可以利用从网络规模预训练中获得的语义知识，并将其应用于机器人问题。当与高度表达性的动作解码机制（如流匹配[8]、扩散[55, 84, 52]或高级动作分词方案[64]）结合时，VLA可以在现实世界中执行各种复杂的操作任务。然而，尽管具有令人印象深刻的语言跟随能力，VLA通常仍在与其训练数据密切匹配的环境中进行评估。虽然一些研究表明，简单的技能（如拿起物体或打开抽屉）可以通过在更广泛的环境中收集机器人数据来实现泛化[14, 67, 28, 49, 64]，但将相同的方法应用于更复杂的、长时程的任务（如清理厨房）是具有挑战性的，因为通过暴力扩展机器人数据收集来充分覆盖可能的情景是不可行的。在我们的实验中，我们在全新的场景中评估π0.5，例如训练中未见过的厨房和卧室，表明我们的VLA不仅通过利用目标移动操作器平台上的直接第一手经验，还通过利用来自其他数据源的信息，能够泛化到全新的场景。这些来源包括来自其他（非移动）机器人的数据、高层语义子任务预测以及来自网络的数据。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Model overview. π0.5\pi_{0.5} is trained in two stages. First, a pre-training stage combines all of the different data sources to produce an initial VLA with discrete tokens. This stage uses data from diverse robotic platforms, high-level semantic action prediction, and data from the web. Robotic data uses the FAST action tokenizer to represent actions as discrete tokens [64]. Second, a post-training stage specializes the model for low-level and high-level inferences for mobile manipulation, leveraging the most task-relevant data, including verbal instructions from human supervisors. This stage uses flow matching to represent the action distribution, enabling efficient real-time inference and the ability to represent fine-grained continuous action sequences. At inference time, the model first infers a high-level subtask, and then predicts the actions based on this subtask.
items.capZh: 图3：模型概述。π0.5分两个阶段训练。首先，预训练阶段将所有不同的数据源结合起来，生成一个具有离散标记的初始VLA。该阶段使用来自多样化机器人平台的数据、高层语义动作预测以及来自网络的数据。机器人数据使用FAST动作分词器将动作表示为离散标记[64]。其次，后训练阶段专门针对移动操作的低层和高层推理优化模型，利用最相关的任务数据，包括来自人类监督员的口头指令。该阶段使用流匹配来表示动作分布，从而实现高效的实时推理和表示细粒度连续动作序列的能力。在推理时，模型首先推断一个高层子任务，然后基于该子任务预测动作。

items.en: Non-robot data co-training. A number of prior works have sought to use diverse non-robot data to improve the generalization of robot policies. Prior methods have explored initializing vision encoders from computer vision datasets [85, 58, 57, 18], or leveraging off-the-shelf task planners [38, 48, 73, 81]. VLA policies are typically initialized from a pre-trained vision-language model, which has been exposed to large amounts of internet vision and language data [23, 92, 42]. Notably, the VLA architecture is flexible and allows to map between input and output sequences of multi-modal vision, language, and action tokens. As such, VLAs broaden the design space of possible transfer approaches beyond simple weight initialization, by supporting the co-training of a single, unified architecture on not just robot action imitation data, but any dataset that interleaves one or multiple of the aforementioned modalities. Prior works have demonstrated that co-training VLAs with data mixtures used for VLM training [23, 92, 86] can improve their generalization ability, e.g., when interacting with new objects or unseen scene backgrounds. In this work, we go beyond VLM data co-training and design a system for co-training VLAs with a broader set of robotics-relevant supervision sources, including data from other robots, high-level semantic subtask predictions, and verbal language instructions. While multitask training and co-training are not new ideas, we show that the specific combination of data sources in our system enables mobile robots to perform complex and long-horizon behaviors in entirely new environments. We believe that this level of generalization, particularly when accounting for the complexity of the tasks, goes significantly beyond the results demonstrated in prior works.
items.zh: 非机器人数据协同训练。许多先前的工作试图利用多样化的非机器人数据来提高机器人策略的泛化能力。先前的方法探索了从计算机视觉数据集初始化视觉编码器[85, 58, 57, 18]，或利用现成的任务规划器[38, 48, 73, 81]。VLA策略通常从预训练的视觉-语言模型初始化，该模型已经接触过大量的互联网视觉和语言数据[23, 92, 42]。值得注意的是，VLA架构是灵活的，允许在多模态视觉、语言和动作标记的输入和输出序列之间进行映射。因此，VLA拓宽了可能的迁移方法的设计空间，超越了简单的权重初始化，支持在统一的架构上不仅对机器人动作模仿数据进行协同训练，而且对任何交错包含上述一种或多种模态的数据集进行协同训练。先前的工作已经证明，使用用于VLM训练的数据混合[23, 92, 86]对VLA进行协同训练可以提高其泛化能力，例如在与新物体或未见过的场景背景交互时。在这项工作中，我们超越了VLM数据协同训练，设计了一个系统，用于对VLA进行更广泛的机器人相关监督源的协同训练，包括来自其他机器人的数据、高层语义子任务预测和口头语言指令。虽然多任务训练和协同训练并非新思想，但我们表明，我们系统中数据源的特定组合使移动机器人能够在全新环境中执行复杂且长时程的行为。我们相信，这种泛化水平，特别是考虑到任务的复杂性时，显著超越了先前工作中展示的结果。

items.en: Robot reasoning and planning with language. A number of prior works have shown that augmenting end-to-end policies with high-level reasoning can significantly improve performance for long-horizon tasks [2, 36, 44, 74, 71, 4, 16, 11, 53, 88, 51, 59, 13, 70, 91, 65, 72, 47, 76, 89], particularly when high-level subtask inference can benefit from large pre-trained LLMs and VLMs. Our method also uses a two-stage inference procedure, where we first infer a high-level semantic subtask (e.g., “pick up the plate”), and then predict the action based on this subtask. Many prior methods have employed two separate models for this purpose, with a VLM predicting semantic steps and a separate low-level policy executing those steps [2, 71, 13, 24, 70, 72, 47]. Our method uses the same exact model for both high-level and low-level inference, in a recipe that more closely resembles chain-of-thought [82] or test-time compute [39] methods, though unlike embodied chain-of-thought methods [88, 46, 61], the high-level inference process still runs at a lower frequency than low-level action inference.
items.zh: 具有语言能力的机器人推理与规划。许多先前的工作表明，用高层推理增强端到端策略可以显著提高长时程任务的性能[2, 36, 44, 74, 71, 4, 16, 11, 53, 88, 51, 59, 13, 70, 91, 65, 72, 47, 76, 89]，特别是当高层子任务推理可以从大型预训练LLM和VLM中受益时。我们的方法也使用两阶段推理过程，首先推断一个高层语义子任务（例如，“拿起盘子”），然后基于该子任务预测动作。许多先前的方法为此目的使用了两个独立的模型，一个VLM预测语义步骤，另一个独立的低层策略执行这些步骤[2, 71, 13, 24, 70, 72, 47]。我们的方法对高层和低层推理使用完全相同的模型，这种方式更类似于思维链[82]或测试时算力[39]方法，尽管与具身思维链方法[88, 46, 61]不同，高层推理过程的运行频率仍然低于低层动作推理。

items.en: Robotic learning systems with open-world generalization. While most robotic learning systems are evaluated in environments that closely match the training data, a number of prior works have explored broader open-world generalization. When the robot’s tasks are restricted to a more narrow set of basic primitives, such as picking up objects, methods that allow for task-specific assumptions (e.g., grasp prediction, or incorporating model-based planning and control) have been shown to generalize broadly, even to entirely new homes [40, 20, 60, 56, 29]. However, such methods do not readily generalize to the full range of possible tasks that a generalist robot might need to perform. More recently, large-scale datasets collected across many domains [41, 68, 63, 67, 14, 49] have been shown to enable generalization of simple but end-to-end learned tasks to new environments [33, 31, 67, 69, 26, 49, 28, 64]. However, the tasks in these demonstrations are still relatively simple, typically less than a minute in length and often with relatively low success rates. We show that π0.5\pi_{0.5} can perform long, multi-stage tasks, such as putting all of the dishes in the sink or picking all of the clothing off the floor of a new bedroom, while generalizing to entirely new homes.
items.zh: 具有开放世界泛化的机器人学习系统。虽然大多数机器人学习系统在与训练数据密切匹配的环境中进行评估，但许多先前的工作探索了更广泛的开放世界泛化。当机器人的任务限制在更狭窄的基本原语集（例如拿起物体）时，允许任务特定假设（例如抓取预测，或结合基于模型的规划和控制）的方法已被证明可以广泛泛化，甚至到全新的家庭[40, 20, 60, 56, 29]。然而，这些方法不容易泛化到通用机器人可能需要执行的全部可能任务。最近，跨多个领域收集的大规模数据集[41, 68, 63, 67, 14, 49]已被证明能够使简单但端到端学习的任务泛化到新环境[33, 31, 67, 69, 26, 49, 28, 64]。然而，这些演示中的任务仍然相对简单，通常长度不到一分钟，并且成功率往往较低。我们展示了π0.5可以执行长的、多阶段的任务，例如将所有盘子放入水槽或从新卧室的地板上捡起所有衣物，同时泛化到全新的家庭。

items.en: Vision-language-action models (VLAs) are typically trained via imitation learning on diverse robot demonstration datasets 𝒟\mathcal{D}, by maximizing the log-likelihood of an action 𝐚t\mathbf{a}_{t} (or, more generally, an action chunk 𝐚t:t+H\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}) given an observation 𝐨t\mathbf{o}_{t} and a natural language task instruction ℓ\ell: maxθ⁡𝔼(𝐚t:t+H,𝐨t,ℓ)∼𝒟​log⁡(πθ​(𝐚t:t+H|𝐨t,ℓ))\max_{\theta}\mathbb{E}_{(\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H},\mathbf{o}_{t},\ell)\sim\mathcal{D}}\log\big{(}\pi_{\theta}(\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}|\mathbf{o}_{t},\ell)\big{)}. The observation typically contains one or more images 𝐈t1,…,𝐈tn\mathbf{I}^{1}_{t},...,\mathbf{I}^{n}_{t} and proprioceptive state 𝐪t\mathbf{q}_{t}, which captures the position of the robot’s joints. VLA architectures follow the design of modern language and vision-language models, with modality-specific tokenizers that map inputs and outputs to discrete (“hard”) or continuous (“soft”) token representations, and a large, auto-regressive transformer backbone that is trained to map from input to output tokens. The weights of these models are initialized from pre-trained vision-language models. By encoding policy inputs and outputs into tokenized representations, the imitation learning problem described above can be cast as a simple next-token-prediction problem over a sequence of observation, instruction and action tokens, and we can leverage the scalable tools of modern machine learning to optimize it. In practice, the choice of tokenizers for image and text inputs follows those of modern vision-language models. For actions, prior work has developed effective, compression-based tokenization approaches [64], which we use in this work during pretraining. A number of recent VLA models have also proposed to represent the action distribution via diffusion [55, 84, 52] or flow matching [8], providing a more expressive representation over continuous-valued action chunks. During the post-training phase of our model, we will build on the design of the π0\pi_{0} model [8], which represents the action distribution via flow matching. In this design, the tokens corresponding to actions receive the partially denoised actions from the previous step of flow matching as input, and output the flow matching vector field. These tokens also use a different set of model weights, which we refer to as an “action expert,” analogously to a mixture of experts architecture. This action expert can specialize to flow matching-based action generation, and can be significantly smaller than the rest of the LLM backbone.
items.zh: 视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）通常通过模仿学习在多样化的机器人演示数据集D上进行训练，通过最大化给定观测ot和自然语言任务指令ℓ的动作at（或更一般地，动作块at:t+H）的对数似然：maxθ E(at:t+H,ot,ℓ)∼D log(πθ(at:t+H|ot,ℓ))。观测通常包含一个或多个图像I1t,...,Int和本体感受状态qt，后者捕获机器人关节的位置。VLA架构遵循现代语言和视觉-语言模型的设计，具有模态特定的分词器，将输入和输出映射到离散（“硬”）或连续（“软”）标记表示，以及一个大型的自回归Transformer主干，训练用于从输入标记映射到输出标记。这些模型的权重从预训练的视觉-语言模型初始化。通过将策略输入和输出编码为标记化表示，上述模仿学习问题可以转化为观测、指令和动作标记序列上的简单下一个词预测问题，我们可以利用现代机器学习的可扩展工具来优化它。在实践中，图像和文本输入的分词器选择遵循现代视觉-语言模型。对于动作，先前的工作开发了有效的、基于压缩的分词方法[64]，我们在预训练期间使用这种方法。许多最近的VLA模型也提出通过扩散[55, 84, 52]或流匹配[8]来表示动作分布，为连续值动作块提供了更具表现力的表示。在我们模型的后训练阶段，我们将基于π0模型[8]的设计，该模型通过流匹配表示动作分布。在这种设计中，对应于动作的标记接收来自流匹配前一步的部分去噪动作作为输入，并输出流匹配向量场。这些标记还使用一组不同的模型权重，我们称之为“动作专家”，类似于混合专家架构。这个动作专家可以专门用于基于流匹配的动作生成，并且可以比LLM主干的其余部分小得多。

full.sec: IV The π0.5\pi_{0.5} Model and Training Recipe

items.en: We provide an overview of the π0.5\pi_{0.5} model and training recipe in Figure 3. The model weights are initialized from a standard VLM trained on data from the web, and training then proceeds in two stages: a pre-training stage intended to adapt the model to diverse robotic tasks, and a post-training stage intended to specialize it to mobile manipulation and equip it with the mechanisms for efficient test-time inference. During pre-training, all tasks, including tasks with robot actions, are represented with discrete tokens, which leads to simple, scalable, and efficient training [64]. During post-training, we adapt the model to also have an action expert, as with π0\pi_{0}, in order to both represent actions with finer granularity and enable more compute-efficient inference for real-time control. At inference-time, the model first produces a high-level subtask for the robot to perform and then, conditioned on this subtask, predicts the low-level actions via the action expert. We describe the model architecture below, followed by a description of each of the phases and their corresponding training tasks.
items.zh: 我们在图3中提供了π0.5模型和训练方案的概述。模型权重从在来自网络的数据上训练的标准VLM初始化，然后训练分两个阶段进行：预训练阶段旨在使模型适应多样化的机器人任务，后训练阶段旨在专门针对移动操作，并为其配备高效的测试时推理机制。在预训练期间，所有任务，包括具有机器人动作的任务，都用离散标记表示，这导致了简单、可扩展和高效的训练[64]。在后训练期间，我们调整模型使其也具有动作专家，如π0，以便以更细的粒度表示动作，并为实时控制实现更计算高效的推理。在推理时，模型首先为机器人产生一个高层子任务，然后在该子任务的条件下，通过动作专家预测低层动作。下面我们描述模型架构，然后描述每个阶段及其相应的训练任务。

items.en: The π0.5\pi_{0.5} architecture can flexibly represent both action chunk distributions and tokenized text outputs, with the latter used both for co-training tasks (e.g., question-answering) and for outputting high-level subtask predictions during hierarchical inference. The distribution captured by the model can be written as πθ​(𝐚t:t+H,ℓ^|𝐨t,ℓ)\pi_{\theta}(\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H},\hat{\ell}|\mathbf{o}_{t},\ell), where 𝐨t=[𝐈t1,…,𝐈tn,𝐪t]\mathbf{o}_{t}=[\mathbf{I}^{1}_{t},...,\mathbf{I}^{n}_{t},\mathbf{q}_{t}] consists of the images from all of the cameras and the robot’s configuration (joint angles, gripper pose, torso lift pose, and base velocity), ℓ\ell is the overall task prompt (e.g., “put away the dishes”), ℓ^\hat{\ell} represents the model’s (tokenized) textual output, which could be either a predicted high-level subtask (e.g., “pick up the plate”) or the answer to a vision-language prompt in web data, and 𝐚t:t+H\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H} is a predicted action chunk. We decompose the distribution as
items.zh: π0.5架构可以灵活地表示动作块分布和标记化文本输出，后者既用于协同训练任务（例如，问答），也用于在层次推理期间输出高层子任务预测。模型捕获的分布可以写为πθ(at:t+H, ℓ̂|ot, ℓ)，其中ot = [I1t, ..., Int, qt]由来自所有摄像头的图像和机器人的配置（关节角度、夹爪位姿、躯干升降位姿和基座速度）组成，ℓ是整体任务提示（例如，“收拾餐具”），ℓ̂表示模型的（标记化的）文本输出，可以是预测的高层子任务（例如，“拿起盘子”）或网络数据中视觉-语言提示的答案，而at:t+H是预测的动作块。我们将分布分解为

items.en: where the action distribution does not depend on ℓ\ell, only on ℓ^\hat{\ell}. Thus, high-level inference captures πθ​(ℓ^|𝐨t,ℓ)\pi_{\theta}(\hat{\ell}|\mathbf{o}_{t},\ell), and low-level inference captures πθ​(𝐚t:t+H|𝐨t,ℓ^)\pi_{\theta}(\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}|\mathbf{o}_{t},\hat{\ell}), with both distributions represented by the same model.
items.zh: 其中动作分布不依赖于ℓ，仅依赖于ℓ̂。因此，高层推理捕获πθ(ℓ̂|ot, ℓ)，低层推理捕获πθ(at:t+H|ot, ℓ̂)，两者都由同一个模型表示。

items.en: The model corresponds to a transformer that takes in NN multimodal input tokens x1:Nx_{1:N} (we use the term token loosely here, referring to both discretized and continuous inputs) and produces a sequence of multimodal outputs y1:Ny_{1:N}, which we can write as y1:N=f​(x1:N,A​(x1:N),ρ​(x1:N))y_{1:N}=f\big{(}x_{1:N},A(x_{1:N}),\rho(x_{1:N})\big{)}. Each xix_{i} can be a text token (xiw∈ℕx_{i}^{w}\in\mathbb{N}), an image patch (xiI∈ℝp×p×3x_{i}^{I}\in\mathbb{R}^{p\times p\times 3}), or an intermediate denoising value of a robot action in flow matching (xia∈ℝdx_{i}^{a}\in\mathbb{R}^{d}). The observations 𝐨t\mathbf{o}_{t} and ℓ\ell form the prefix part of x1:Nx_{1:N}. Depending on the token type, as indicated by ρ​(xi)\rho(x_{i}), each token can be processed not only by a different encoder, but also by different expert weights within the transformer. For example, image patches are fed through a vision encoder, and text tokens are embedded with an embedding matrix. Following π0\pi_{0} [8], we linearly project action tokens xiax_{i}^{a} into the transformer embedding space and use separate expert weights in the transformer to process the action tokens. The attention matrix A​(x1:N)∈[0,1]N×NA(x_{1:N})\in[0,1]^{N\times N} indicates if a token can attend to another token. Compared to standard causal attention in LLMs, image patch, textual prompt, and continuous action tokens use bidirectional attention.
items.zh: 该模型对应于一个Transformer，它接收N个多模态输入标记x1:N（我们在此松散地使用术语“标记”，指代离散化和连续输入）并产生一个多模态输出序列y1:N，我们可以将其写为y1:N = f(x1:N, A(x1:N), ρ(x1:N))。每个xi可以是文本标记（xwi ∈ N）、图像块（xIi ∈ Rp×p×3）或流匹配中机器人动作的中间去噪值（xai ∈ Rd）。观测ot和ℓ构成x1:N的前缀部分。根据标记类型（由ρ(xi)指示），每个标记不仅可以由不同的编码器处理，还可以由Transformer内不同的专家权重处理。例如，图像块通过视觉编码器馈送，文本标记通过嵌入矩阵嵌入。遵循π0 [8]，我们将动作标记xai线性投影到Transformer嵌入空间，并使用Transformer中独立的专家权重来处理动作标记。注意力矩阵A(x1:N) ∈ [0,1]N×N指示一个标记是否可以关注另一个标记。与LLM中的标准因果注意力相比，图像块、文本提示和连续动作标记使用双向注意力。

items.en: As we want our model to output both text (to answer questions about the scene or to output next tasks to accomplish) and actions (to act in the world), the output of ff is split into text token logits and action output tokens, respectively (y1:Mℓ,y1:Ha)\big{(}y^{\ell}_{1:M},y^{a}_{1:H}\big{)}. The first MM correspond to text token logits that can be used to sample ℓ^\hat{\ell} and the later HH tokens are produced by a separate action expert, as in π0\pi_{0}, and projected via a linear mapping to continuous outputs used to obtain 𝐚t:t+H\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H} (see next section). Note that M+H≤NM+H\leq N, i.e., not all outputs are associated with a loss. The robot proprioceptive state is discretized and input to the model as text tokens. More details about the architecture are in Appendix A-E.
items.zh: 由于我们希望我们的模型同时输出文本（以回答关于场景的问题或输出下一个要完成的任务）和动作（以在世界中行动），f的输出被分为文本标记logits和动作输出标记，分别为(yℓ1:M, ya1:H)。前M个对应于可用于采样ℓ̂的文本标记logits，后H个标记由独立的动作专家产生，如π0中，并通过线性映射投影到连续输出，用于获得at:t+H（见下一节）。注意M+H ≤ N，即并非所有输出都与损失相关联。机器人本体感受状态被离散化并作为文本标记输入模型。关于架构的更多细节见附录A-E。

full.sec: IV-B Combining discrete & continuous action representations
full.secZh: 四-B 离散与连续动作表示的融合

items.capEn: Figure 4: Examples from pre-training and post-training tasks. π0.5\pi_{0.5} is pre-trained on data from mobile manipulators (MM), non-mobile robots in diverse environments (ME), and cross-embodiment data collected under laboratory conditions (CE), as well as high-level subtask prediction (HL), and multi-modal web data (WD). In a post-training phase, we additionally use verbal instructions (VI), and omit the laboratory cross-embodiment data (CE) to focus the model on mobile manipulation and diverse environments. The figure displays an exemplary subset of the tasks in each category.
items.capZh: 图4：预训练和后训练任务示例。π0.5在来自移动操作器（MM）、多样化环境中的非移动机器人（ME）和实验室条件下收集的跨本体数据（CE）以及高层子任务预测（HL）和多模态网络数据（WD）上进行预训练。在后训练阶段，我们额外使用口头指令（VI），并省略实验室跨本体数据（CE），以使模型专注于移动操作和多样化环境。该图展示了每个类别中任务的示例性子集。

items.en: Similarly to π0\pi_{0}, we use flow-matching [50] to predict continuous actions in the final model. Given 𝐚t:t+Hτ,ω=τ​𝐚t:t+H+(1−τ)​ω\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}^{\tau,\omega}=\tau\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}+(1-\tau)\omega, ω∼𝒩​(0,𝐈)\omega\sim\mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{I}), where τ∈[0,1]\tau\in[0,1] is the flow matching time index, the model is trained to predict the flow vector field ω−𝐚t\omega-\mathbf{a}_{t}. However, as shown in [64], VLA training can be much faster when actions are represented by discrete tokens, particularly when using a tokenization scheme that is efficient for compressing the action chunks (e.g., FAST). Unfortunately, such discrete representations are less well-suited for real-time inference, because they require expensive autoregressive decoding for inference [64]. Therefore, an ideal model design would train on discretized actions but still allow for use of flow matching to produce continuous actions at inference time.
items.zh: 与 π0 类似，我们使用流匹配 [50] 在最终模型中预测连续动作。给定 a_{t:t+H}^{τ,ω} = τ a_{t:t+H} + (1-τ)ω，其中 ω ~ N(0, I)，τ ∈ [0,1] 是流匹配时间索引，模型被训练来预测流向量场 ω - a_t。然而，如 [64] 所示，当动作由离散 token 表示时，VLA 训练可以快得多，特别是当使用对压缩动作块高效的 token 化方案（例如 FAST）时。不幸的是，这种离散表示不太适合实时推理，因为它们需要昂贵的自回归解码进行推理 [64]。因此，理想的模型设计将在离散化动作上训练，但仍允许在推理时使用流匹配生成连续动作。

items.en: Our model is therefore trained to predict actions both through autoregressive sampling of tokens (using the FAST tokenizer) and iterative integration of the flow field, combining the best of both worlds. We use the attention matrix to ensure that the different action representations do not attend to each other. Our model is optimized to minimize the combined loss
items.zh: 因此，我们的模型被训练来同时通过 token 的自回归采样（使用 FAST token 化器）和流场的迭代积分来预测动作，结合了两者的优点。我们使用注意力矩阵来确保不同的动作表示不会相互关注。我们的模型被优化以最小化组合损失

items.en: where H​(x1:M,y1:Mℓ)H(x_{1:M},y^{\ell}_{1:M}) is the cross entropy loss between the text tokens and predicted logits (including the FAST encoded action tokens), y1:Ha=fθa​(𝐚t:t+Hτ,ω,𝐨t,ℓ)y^{a}_{1:H}=f^{a}_{\theta}(\mathbf{a}^{\tau,\omega}_{t:t+H},\mathbf{o}_{t},\ell) is the output from the (smaller) action expert, and α∈ℝ\alpha\in\mathbb{R} is a trade-off parameter. This scheme enables us to first pre-train our model as a standard VLM transformer model by mapping actions to text tokens (α=0\alpha=0), and then add additional action expert weights predicting continuous action tokens in a non-autoregressive fashion for fast inference in a post-training stage. We find that following this procedure, which is further explained below, leads to stable pre-training and excellent language following abilities of the VLA model. At inference time we then use standard autoregressive decoding for text tokens ℓ^\hat{\ell} followed by 1010 denoising steps, conditioned on text tokens, to produce actions 𝐚t:t+H\mathbf{a}_{t:t+H}.
items.zh: 其中 H(x_{1:M}, y^ℓ_{1:M}) 是文本 token 与预测 logits（包括 FAST 编码的动作 token）之间的交叉熵损失，y^a_{1:H} = f^a_θ(a^{τ,ω}_{t:t+H}, o_t, ℓ) 是来自（较小的）动作专家的输出，α ∈ R 是一个权衡参数。这种方案使我们能够首先通过将动作映射到文本 token（α=0）将我们的模型预训练为标准 VLM Transformer 模型，然后在后训练阶段添加额外的动作专家权重，以非自回归方式预测连续动作 token，以实现快速推理。我们发现，遵循这一程序（下文将进一步解释）可以实现稳定的预训练和 VLA 模型出色的语言跟随能力。在推理时，我们随后使用标准自回归解码生成文本 token ℓ̂，然后进行 10 个去噪步骤，以文本 token 为条件，生成动作 a_{t:t+H}。

items.en: In the first training stage, π0.5\pi_{0.5} is trained with a broad range of robot and non-robot data, which we summarize below and illustrate in Figure 4. It is trained as a standard auto-regressive transformer, performing next-token prediction of text, object locations, and FAST encoded action tokens.
items.zh: 在第一个训练阶段，π0.5 使用广泛的机器人和非机器人数据进行训练，我们在下面总结并在图 4 中说明。它被训练为标准自回归 Transformer，执行文本、对象位置和 FAST 编码动作 token 的下一个词预测。

items.en: Diverse Mobile Manipulator data (MM). We use about 400 hours of data of mobile manipulators performing household tasks in about 100 different home environments, some of which are shown in Figure 7, using the robots in Section IV-E. This slice of the training set is the most directly relevant to our evaluation tasks, which consist of similar cleaning and tidying tasks in new, unseen, home environments.
items.zh: 多样化移动操作器数据（MM）。我们使用了约 400 小时的数据，这些数据来自移动操作器在大约 100 个不同家庭环境中执行家务任务，其中一些如图 7 所示，使用了第 IV-E 节中的机器人。这部分训练集与我们评估任务最直接相关，这些任务包括在新的、未见过的家庭环境中进行类似的清洁和整理任务。

items.en: Diverse Multi-Environment non-mobile robot data (ME). We also collected non-mobile robot data, either with a single arm or two arms, in a variety of home environments. These arms were fixed to surfaces or mounting platforms, and because they are significantly lighter and easier to transport, we were able to gather a more diverse dataset in a wider range of homes with them. However, this ME data comes from a different embodiment than the mobile robots.
items.zh: 多样化多环境非移动机器人数据（ME）。我们还收集了非移动机器人数据，无论是单臂还是双臂，在各种家庭环境中。这些手臂固定在表面或安装平台上，由于它们明显更轻且易于运输，我们能够用它们在更广泛的家庭中收集更多样化的数据集。然而，这些 ME 数据来自与移动机器人不同的具身。

items.en: Cross-Embodiment laboratory data (CE). We collected data for a wide range of tasks (e.g., bussing a table, folding shirts) in the laboratory, with simpler tabletop environments and a variety of robot types. Some of these tasks are highly relevant to our evaluation (e.g., putting dishes in a bin), while others are not (e.g., grinding coffee beans). This data includes single-arm and dual-arm manipulators, and both static and mobile bases. We also include the open-source OXE dataset [15]. This dataset is an extended version of the dataset used by π0\pi_{0}[8].
items.zh: 跨具身实验室数据（CE）。我们在实验室中收集了广泛任务的数据（例如，清理餐桌、折叠衬衫），环境为更简单的桌面环境，并使用了多种机器人类型。其中一些任务与我们的评估高度相关（例如，将盘子放入箱子），而其他任务则不相关（例如，研磨咖啡豆）。这些数据包括单臂和双臂操作器，以及静态和移动底座。我们还包含了开源 OXE 数据集 [15]。该数据集是 π0 [8] 所用数据集的扩展版本。

items.en: High-Level subtask prediction (HL). Breaking down high-level task commands such as “clean the bedroom” into shorter subtasks like “adjust the blanket” and “pick up pillow”, similar to chain-of-thought prompting for language models, can help a trained policy reason about the current scene and better determine the next action. For robot data in MM, ME, and CE where the task involves multiple subtasks, we manually annotate all data with semantic descriptions of the subtasks and train π0.5\pi_{0.5} to jointly predict the subtask labels (as text) as well as the actions (conditioned on the subtask label) based on the current observation and high-level command. This naturally leads to a model that can act both as a high-level policy (outputting subtasks) and low-level policy that executes actions for these subtasks. We also label relevant bounding boxes shown in the current observation and train π0.5\pi_{0.5} to predict them before predicting the subtask.
items.zh: 高级子任务预测（HL）。将高级任务命令（如“清洁卧室”）分解为较短的子任务（如“整理毯子”和“捡起枕头”），类似于语言模型的思维链提示，可以帮助训练好的策略推理当前场景并更好地确定下一个动作。对于 MM、ME 和 CE 中涉及多个子任务的机器人数据，我们手动用子任务的语义描述注释所有数据，并训练 π0.5 基于当前观察和高级命令联合预测子任务标签（作为文本）和动作（以子任务标签为条件）。这自然导致了一个模型，既可以作为高级策略（输出子任务），也可以作为执行这些子任务动作的低级策略。我们还标记了当前观察中显示的相关边界框，并训练 π0.5 在预测子任务之前预测它们。

items.en: Multi-modal Web Data (WD). Finally we include a diverse set of web data involving image captioning (CapsFusion [87], COCO [12]), question answering (Cambrian-7M [77], PixMo [19], VQAv2 [32]), and object localization in pre-training. For object localization, we further extend the standard datasets with additional web data of indoor scenes and household objects with bounding box annotations.
items.zh: 多模态网络数据（WD）。最后，我们包含了一系列多样的网络数据，涉及图像描述（CapsFusion [87], COCO [12]）、问答（Cambrian-7M [77], PixMo [19], VQAv2 [32]）和预训练中的对象定位。对于对象定位，我们进一步用带有边界框注释的室内场景和家庭对象的额外网络数据扩展了标准数据集。

items.en: For all action data, we train the model to predict target joint and end-effector poses. To differentiate the two, we add ‘<<control_mode>> joint/end effector <<control_mode>>’ to the text prompt. All action data is normalized to [−1,1][-1,1] using the 1% and 99% quantile of each action dimension of the individual dataset. We set the dimensionality of the action 𝐚\mathbf{a} to a fixed number to accommodate the largest action space among all the datasets. For robots with lower-dimensional configuration and action spaces, we zero-pad the action vectors.
items.zh: 对于所有动作数据，我们训练模型预测目标关节和末端执行器位姿。为了区分两者，我们在文本提示中添加了 '<<control_mode>> joint/end effector <<control_mode>>'。所有动作数据使用各个数据集每个动作维度的 1% 和 99% 分位数归一化到 [-1,1]。我们将动作 a 的维度设置为固定数字，以适应所有数据集中最大的动作空间。对于配置和动作空间维度较低的机器人，我们对动作向量进行零填充。

items.en: After pre-training the model with discrete tokens for 280k gradient steps, we perform a second stage of training that we refer to as post-training. The purpose of this stage is to both specialize the model to our use-case (mobile manipulation in homes), and to add an action expert that can produce continuous action chunks via flow matching. This stage jointly trains with next-token prediction, to preserve text prediction capabilities, and flow matching for the action expert (which is initialized with random weights at the beginning of post-training). We optimize the objective in Equation (1), with α=10.0\alpha=10.0 for 80k additional steps. The post-training action dataset consists of the MM and ME robot data, filtered down to successful episodes that are below a fixed length threshold. We include web data (WD) to preserve the model’s semantic and visual capabilities, and the slice of HL data corresponding to the multi-environment datasets. Additionally, to improve the model’s ability to predict appropriate high-level subtasks, we collect verbal instruction demonstrations (VI), which are constructed by expert users providing “language demonstrations,” selecting appropriate sub-task commands to command the robot to perform mobile manipulation tasks step by step. These examples are collected by “teleoperating” the robot in real time with language to perform tasks with the learned low level policy, essentially providing demonstrations of good high-level subtask outputs for a trained policy.
items.zh: 在使用离散 token 预训练模型 280k 梯度步后，我们进行第二阶段训练，称为后训练。此阶段的目的是使模型专门用于我们的用例（家庭中的移动操作），并添加一个动作专家，该专家可以通过流匹配生成连续动作块。此阶段联合训练下一个词预测（以保持文本预测能力）和动作专家的流匹配（在后训练开始时用随机权重初始化）。我们优化方程 (1) 中的目标，α=10.0，进行额外的 80k 步。后训练动作数据集由 MM 和 ME 机器人数据组成，过滤到成功且低于固定长度阈值的片段。我们包含网络数据（WD）以保持模型的语义和视觉能力，以及对应于多环境数据集的 HL 数据部分。此外，为了提高模型预测适当高级子任务的能力，我们收集了口头指令演示（VI），这些演示由专家用户提供“语言演示”，选择适当的子任务命令来逐步命令机器人执行移动操作任务。这些示例通过用语言“远程操作”机器人实时执行任务来收集，使用已学习的低级策略，本质上为训练好的策略提供了良好高级子任务输出的演示。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Robot system overview. We use two mobile manipulator platforms – each has four cameras (forward, backward, and both wrists), two 6 DoF arms with parallel jaw grippers, a mobile base, and a torso lift mechanism. The π0.5\pi_{0.5} model controls the joints and grippers of each arm, base velocity, and the lift position, resulting in 18-19 DoF state and action spaces.
items.capZh: 图 5：机器人系统概览。我们使用两种移动操作平台——每个都有四个摄像头（前、后和两个手腕）、两个带平行爪夹持器的 6 自由度手臂、一个移动底座和一个躯干升降机构。π0.5 模型控制每个手臂的关节和夹持器、底座速度和升降位置，产生 18-19 自由度的状态和动作空间。

full.secZh: 四-E 机器人系统细节

items.en: The robot systems used in our mobile manipulation experiments are illustrated in Figure 5. We conducted all of our experiments using two types of mobile manipulators. Both platforms are equipped with two 6 DoF arms with parallel jaw grippers and wrist-mounted monocular RGB cameras, a wheeled holonomic base, and a torso lift mechanism. The state and action spaces for the base correspond to linear (2D) and angular (1D) velocity, and the torso lift mechanism is either 1D (up/down) or 2D (up/down and forward/backward). In addition to the two wrist cameras, the robots have a forward and backward facing camera mounted between the arms. We use all four cameras for high-level inference, and the wrist and forward cameras for the low-level inference process. The total dimensionality of the state and action spaces is 18 or 19, depending on the platform.
items.zh: 我们的移动操作实验中使用的机器人系统如图 5 所示。我们使用两种类型的移动操作器进行所有实验。两个平台都配备了两个带平行爪夹持器的 6 自由度手臂和腕部单目 RGB 摄像头、一个轮式全向底座和一个躯干升降机构。底座的状态和动作空间对应线性（2D）和角速度（1D），躯干升降机构是 1D（上/下）或 2D（上/下和前/后）。除了两个腕部摄像头，机器人在手臂之间还有一个前向和后向摄像头。我们使用所有四个摄像头进行高级推理，使用腕部和前向摄像头进行低级推理过程。状态和动作空间的总维度为 18 或 19，具体取决于平台。

items.en: The control system is very simple: the π0.5\pi_{0.5} model directly commands target poses for the arms, gripper, and torso lift, and the target base velocities at 50 Hz (with action chunking). These targets are tracked with simple PD controllers, without any additional trajectory planning or collision detection. All manipulation and navigation control is fully end-to-end.
items.zh: 控制系统非常简单：π0.5 模型直接以 50 Hz（带动作分块）命令手臂、夹持器和躯干升降的目标位姿以及目标底座速度。这些目标通过简单的 PD 控制器进行跟踪，无需任何额外的轨迹规划或碰撞检测。所有操作和导航控制都是完全端到端的。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Evaluation environments. We evaluate π0.5\pi_{0.5} in entirely new kitchens and bedrooms that were not seen during training, with novel objects, backgrounds, and layouts. We use a set of mock rooms for controlled, reproducible quantitative comparisons (left) and real homes for a realistic final evaluation (right).
items.capZh: 图 6：评估环境。我们在训练期间未见过的全新厨房和卧室中评估 π0.5，这些环境具有新颖的物体、背景和布局。我们使用一组模拟房间进行可控、可重复的定量比较（左），并在真实家庭中进行逼真的最终评估（右）。

items.en: The π0.5\pi_{0.5} model is designed to generalize broadly to new environments. While it is common to evaluate VLAs in environments that match the training data, we conduct all of our experiments in novel environments that were not seen in training. For quantitative comparisons, we use a set of mock home environments to provide a controlled and reproducible setup, while the most realistic final evaluation is conducted in three real homes that were not part of the training set (see Figure 6). Our experiments focus on the following questions:
items.zh: π0.5 模型旨在广泛泛化到新环境。虽然通常在匹配训练数据的环境中评估 VLA，但我们在训练中未见过的全新环境中进行所有实验。对于定量比较，我们使用一组模拟家庭环境以提供可控且可重复的设置，而最逼真的最终评估在三个不属于训练集的真实家庭中进行（见图 6）。我们的实验关注以下问题：

items.en: Can π0.5\pi_{0.5} effectively generalize to complex multi-stage tasks in entirely new homes?
items.zh: π0.5 能否有效泛化到全新家庭中的复杂多阶段任务？

items.en: How does the generalization of π0.5\pi_{0.5} scale with the number of distinct environments in the training data?
items.zh: π0.5 的泛化如何随训练数据中不同环境的数量而扩展？

items.en: How do the individual co-training ingredients in the π0.5\pi_{0.5} training mixture contribute to its final performance?
items.zh: π0.5 训练混合中的各个协同训练成分如何对其最终性能做出贡献？

items.en: How does π0.5\pi_{0.5} compare to the π0\pi_{0} VLA?
items.zh: π0.5 与 π0 VLA 相比如何？

items.en: How important is the high-level inference component of π0.5\pi_{0.5}, and how does it compare to flat, low-level inference as well as oracle high-level baselines?
items.zh: π0.5的高层推理组件有多重要？与平面低层推理以及oracle高层基线相比如何？

full.sec: V-A Can π0.5\pi_{0.5} generalize to real homes?
full.secZh: 五-A π0.5能否泛化到真实家庭环境？

items.capEn: (a) Example rollouts. We visualize an exemplary π0.5\pi_{0.5} episode for one task from each home. Top to bottom: putting items in a drawer in Home 1, followed by putting dishes in the sink in Home 2, and putting clothes in the laundry basket in Home 3. The human instruction for each is given on the left, and the high-level subtask prediction from π0.5\pi_{0.5} is shown beneath each frame in blue.
items.capZh: (a) 示例轨迹。我们展示了每个家庭中一个任务的典型π0.5回合。从上到下：在家庭1中将物品放入抽屉，接着在家庭2中将盘子放入水槽，以及在家庭3中将衣服放入洗衣篮。左侧给出了人类指令，蓝色文字显示了π0.5在每个帧下方的高层子任务预测。

items.capEn: (b) Quantitative evaluation. We show the task progress per task and environment averaged over 10 trials. We find that π0.5\pi_{0.5}’s performance in the mock evaluation setups is representative of its performance in real homes.
items.capZh: (b) 定量评估。我们展示了每个任务和环境在10次试验中的任务进展。我们发现π0.5在模拟评估设置中的表现能够代表其在真实家庭中的表现。

items.en: To answer Question (1), we evaluated π0.5\pi_{0.5} in three real homes that were not present in the training set, using both types of robots. In each of the homes, the robots were instructed to perform a bedroom and kitchen cleaning task. The evaluation rubrics for each task are provided in Appendix A-B and roughly correspond to the percentage of steps in each task that were completed successfully (e.g., placing half the dishes in the sink corresponds to around 50%). The results in Figure 7 show that π0.5\pi_{0.5} was able to consistently succeed on a variety of tasks in each home (we note that, additionally, the model is capable of performing many more tasks than used in our quantitative evaluation). Many of the tasks involve multiple stages (e.g., moving multiple objects) lasting about 2 to 5 minutes. For these trials, the model is provided with a simple high-level command (e.g., “place the dishes in the sink”), and the high-level inference process autonomously determines appropriate steps (e.g., “pick up the cup”). This level of in-the-wild generalization goes significantly beyond the results demonstrated with prior vision-language-action models, both in terms of the degree of novelty that the model must handle, and the task duration and complexity.
items.zh: 为了回答问题(1)，我们在三个未出现在训练集中的真实家庭中评估了π0.5，使用了两种类型的机器人。在每个家庭中，机器人被指示执行卧室和厨房清洁任务。每个任务的评估标准在附录A-B中提供，大致对应于每个任务中成功完成的步骤百分比（例如，将一半盘子放入水槽对应约50%）。图7中的结果表明，π0.5能够在每个家庭中持续成功完成各种任务（我们注意到，此外，该模型能够执行比我们定量评估中使用的更多任务）。许多任务涉及多个阶段（例如，移动多个物体），持续约2到5分钟。在这些试验中，模型被提供一个简单的高层指令（例如，“将盘子放入水槽”），高层推理过程自主确定适当的步骤（例如，“拿起杯子”）。这种野外泛化水平显著超越了先前视觉-语言-动作模型所展示的结果，无论是在模型必须处理的新颖程度，还是在任务持续时间和复杂性方面。

full.sec: V-B How does generalization scale with the number of scenes?
full.secZh: 五-B 泛化能力如何随场景数量扩展？

items.en: In the next set of experiments, we aim to measure how generalization scales with the number of environments seen in the training data. We vary the number of environments in the mobile manipulation data and measure its impact on generalization by training with data from 3, 12, 22, 53, 82, and 104 locations. Since applying the entire pre-training and post-training recipe to each of these datasets is prohibitively compute-intensive, for these experiments we pre-train on the mixture of robot action prediction data without mobile manipulation data, and then compare models post-trained on datasets that comprise mobile manipulation data from varying numbers of environments. While the datasets split by location in principle differ in size, in practice the number of training steps (40k) is chosen such that each model sees the same number of unique data samples, which allows us to control for dataset size when varying the number of locations used within a post-training experiment.
items.zh: 在接下来的实验中，我们旨在衡量泛化能力如何随训练数据中环境数量的增加而扩展。我们改变移动操作数据中的环境数量，并通过使用来自3、12、22、53、82和104个地点的数据训练，衡量其对泛化的影响。由于对每个数据集应用完整的预训练和后训练流程在计算上过于昂贵，在这些实验中，我们在没有移动操作数据的情况下对机器人动作预测数据的混合进行预训练，然后比较在包含来自不同数量环境的移动操作数据的数据集上进行后训练的模型。虽然按地点划分的数据集在大小上原则上不同，但实际上训练步数（40k）被选择为使得每个模型看到相同数量的唯一数据样本，这允许我们在后训练实验中改变使用的地点数量时控制数据集大小。

items.en: Each model is evaluated in the mock environments shown in Figure 6, which are not seen in training. We conduct two types of evaluations. First, to evaluate overall performance on multi-stage tasks, we use the standard rubric in Appendix A-B and the mock test homes to evaluate each model’s end-to-end performance on putting dishes in the sink, packing items into a drawer, putting away laundry, and making a bed. Second, we conduct a more fine-grained evaluation of each model’s ability to follow language instructions and interact with novel objects, where the robot must pick up specific objects from a kitchen counter based on language commands. These experiments use both in-distribution objects from similar categories as those in the training data (but new instances), as well as out-of-distribution objects from unseen categories. The latter necessitates broad semantic generalization.
items.zh: 每个模型在图6所示的模拟环境中进行评估，这些环境在训练中未见。我们进行两种类型的评估。首先，为了评估多阶段任务的整体性能，我们使用附录A-B中的标准评估标准和模拟测试家庭，评估每个模型在将盘子放入水槽、将物品打包进抽屉、整理衣物和整理床铺任务上的端到端性能。其次，我们对每个模型遵循语言指令和与新颖物体交互的能力进行更细粒度的评估，其中机器人必须根据语言指令从厨房台面上拿起特定物体。这些实验使用了来自训练数据中类似类别的分布内物体（但新实例），以及来自未见类别的分布外物体。后者需要广泛的语义泛化。

items.capEn: Figure 8: Evaluating performance with different numbers of locations. Performance over the four test tasks — “dishes in sink”, “items in drawer”, “laundry basket”, “make bed” — improves with more training environments. The dashed green line and green bar show a baseline model that includes the test homes in the training set. Compared to this model, our best model achieves similar performance, despite not seeing any data from the test homes.
items.capZh: 图8：评估不同地点数量下的性能。四个测试任务——“盘子放入水槽”、“物品放入抽屉”、“洗衣篮”、“整理床铺”——的性能随着训练环境的增加而提高。虚线绿线和绿色条显示了一个包含测试家庭在训练集中的基线模型。与此模型相比，我们的最佳模型实现了相似的性能，尽管没有看到任何来自测试家庭的数据。

items.en: The results of the first experiment are shown in Figure 8. The average performance among the tasks generally improves with more training locations. To quantify how much the final model (with 104 locations) bridges the generalization gap, we include a control (shown in green) that is trained directly on data from the test homes. This control attains similar performance as the final 104-location model, suggesting that our co-training recipe effectively enables broad generalization, reaching similar performance to a model trained on the test environment. To confirm that this generalization performance requires our full co-training recipe, we additionally include two baselines that do not use any of the other co-training tasks in the pre-training phase, but instead train directly on either data from the test environment (light green) or mobile manipulation data from the 104 training locations (light yellow). The performance for both those baselines is significantly worse — this indicates that the other data sources leveraged by our full training recipe are essential for good generalization, even when the policy has seen robot data from test homes. When not using data from test homes, pre-training with our recipe is especially important, as can be seen by the large gap between the green bars and light yellow bar in Figure 8.
items.zh: 第一个实验的结果如图8所示。任务的平均性能通常随着训练地点的增加而提高。为了量化最终模型（使用104个地点）弥合泛化差距的程度，我们包含了一个对照（以绿色显示），该对照直接在测试家庭的数据上训练。该对照达到了与最终104地点模型相似的性能，表明我们的协同训练配方有效地实现了广泛泛化，达到了与在测试环境上训练的模型相似的性能。为了确认这种泛化性能需要我们的完整协同训练配方，我们还包含了两个基线，它们在预训练阶段不使用任何其他协同训练任务，而是直接在测试环境的数据（浅绿色）或来自104个训练地点的移动操作数据（浅黄色）上训练。这两个基线的性能都显著更差——这表明即使策略已经看到了来自测试家庭的机器人数据，我们的完整训练配方所利用的其他数据源对于良好的泛化也是必不可少的。当不使用测试家庭的数据时，使用我们的配方进行预训练尤其重要，这可以从图8中绿色条和浅黄色条之间的巨大差距看出。

items.capEn: Figure 9: Evaluating language following with different numbers of training locations. We evaluate language following rate and success rate for picking up user-indicated items and placing them into drawers or sinks, averaged over seen object categories (“in-distribution”) or unseen categories (“out-of-distribution”). Performance increases steadily as we increase the number of training locations.
items.capZh: 图9：评估不同训练地点数量下的语言遵循能力。我们评估了语言遵循率和成功率，即拿起用户指示的物品并将其放入抽屉或水槽，平均分布在见过的物体类别（“分布内”）或未见过的类别（“分布外”）上。随着训练地点数量的增加，性能稳步提升。

items.en: The results of the second experiment (language following) are shown in Figure 9. We report the language following rate, which measures how often the robot selects the object indicated in the language command, and success rate, which measures how often the robot successfully places that object in the correct location (either inside the drawer or inside the sink, depending on the test scenario). We separately measure performance on object categories seen in training (but new object instances) and unseen (“out-of-distribution”) object categories. Details of this experiment are shown and discussed in Appendix A-C. Figure 9 shows that, as the number of locations in the training data increases, both language following performance and success rate improve. As expected, the performance on in-distribution objects improves more quickly than that of out-of-distribution objects. As each new environment introduces new household items, the model becomes generally more robust and starts to generalize to task categories that were not present in the training data.
items.zh: 第二个实验（语言遵循）的结果如图9所示。我们报告了语言遵循率，衡量机器人选择语言指令中指示的物体的频率，以及成功率，衡量机器人成功将该物体放置在正确位置（根据测试场景，放入抽屉或水槽）的频率。我们分别测量了训练中见过的物体类别（但新物体实例）和未见过的（“分布外”）物体类别的性能。该实验的细节在附录A-C中展示和讨论。图9显示，随着训练数据中地点数量的增加，语言遵循性能和成功率都得到提高。正如预期，分布内物体的性能比分布外物体提升得更快。随着每个新环境引入新的家居物品，模型变得更加鲁棒，并开始泛化到训练数据中未出现的任务类别。

full.sec: V-C How important is each part of our co-training recipe?
full.secZh: 五-C 协同训练方案各部分的重要性？

items.en: To study Question (3), we compare our full π0.5\pi_{0.5} model to other training mixtures to study the importance of each mixture component, again using end-to-end task performance in the mock homes and the language following evaluation described in Section V-B. As a reminder, our full recipe uses data from mobile manipulators in many environments (MM), static manipulators in many environments (ME), and diverse cross-embodiment data collected in laboratory settings (CE). It also includes high-level data where the prediction corresponds to a high-level language command (HL), and web data corresponding to captioning, VQA, and object localization tasks (WD). Post-training also uses verbal instruction data (VI), which we analyze in Section V-E. In these experiments, we ablate different parts of the mixture:
items.zh: 为了研究问题(3)，我们将完整的π0.5模型与其他训练混合进行比较，以研究每个混合成分的重要性，再次使用模拟家庭中的端到端任务性能和第五节B中描述的语言遵循评估。提醒一下，我们的完整配方使用了来自多种环境中移动操作器（MM）、多种环境中静态操作器（ME）以及实验室环境中收集的多样化跨具身数据（CE）的数据。它还包括高层数据，其中预测对应高层语言指令（HL），以及对应于字幕、VQA和物体定位任务的网络数据（WD）。后训练还使用口头指令数据（VI），我们在第五节E中分析。在这些实验中，我们消融了混合的不同部分：

items.en: no ME: this ablation excludes multi-environment non-mobile data.
items.zh: 无ME：此消融排除了多环境非移动数据。

items.en: no CE: this ablation excludes the laboratory cross-embodiment data.
items.zh: 无CE：此消融排除了实验室跨具身数据。

items.en: no ME or CE: this ablation excludes both data sources from other robots, such that the model is trained on only data from the target mobile manipulator platform as well as web data.
items.zh: 无ME或CE：此消融排除了来自其他机器人的两个数据源，使得模型仅使用目标移动操作器平台的数据以及网络数据进行训练。

items.capEn: Figure 10: Training recipe ablations, mock homes. We evaluate variants of our model that exclude different parts of the training mixture on all four test tasks (10 trials per policy and task). Including cross-embodiment data, both in diverse environments (ME) and for diverse tasks in laboratory settings (CE) is important for good performance, with large degradation when either or both of these data sources are removed. Web data (WD) does not make a significant difference in these experiments, but we will see in Figures 11 and 13 that it impacts object generalization and high-level performance.
items.capZh: 图10：训练配方消融，模拟家庭。我们评估了排除训练混合不同部分的模型变体在所有四个测试任务上的表现（每个策略和任务10次试验）。包含跨具身数据，无论是在多样化环境中（ME）还是在实验室环境中的多样化任务（CE），对于良好性能都很重要，当移除这些数据源中的一个或两个时，性能大幅下降。网络数据（WD）在这些实验中没有显著差异，但我们将在图11和13中看到它影响物体泛化和高层性能。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Training recipe ablations, language following. Evaluating language following with in-distribution and out-of-distribution objects after training on different numbers of locations. Including web data (WD) is important for out-of-distribution (OOD) performance in particular. Cross-embodiment (CE) and diverse environment (ME) data both have a large impact on in-distribution and out-of-distribution performance.
items.capZh: 图11：训练配方消融，语言遵循。评估在不同地点数量训练后，对分布内和分布外物体的语言遵循能力。包含网络数据（WD）对于分布外（OOD）性能尤其重要。跨具身（CE）和多样化环境（ME）数据都对分布内和分布外性能有较大影响。

items.en: The results on the full mock home tasks are shown in Figure 10 (detailed breakdown of performance on each task in Appendix A-D). First, we see in the results that excluding either of the two cross-embodiment data sources (ME and CE) significantly degrades performance, indicating that π0.5\pi_{0.5} benefits considerably from cross-embodiment transfer, from both other environments (ME) and other tasks (CE). Excluding both sources harms performance even more. Interestingly, the difference in performance with the no WD ablation is not statistically significant in this experiment, though we show later that web data has a large impact on language following (below) and high-level subtask inference (Section V-E).
items.zh: 完整模拟家庭任务的结果如图10所示（每个任务性能的详细分解见附录A-D）。首先，我们从结果中看到，排除两个跨具身数据源（ME和CE）中的任何一个都会显著降低性能，表明π0.5从跨具身迁移中获益良多，无论是来自其他环境（ME）还是其他任务（CE）。同时排除两个源对性能的损害更大。有趣的是，无WD消融的性能差异在此实验中统计上不显著，尽管我们稍后展示网络数据对语言遵循（下文）和高层子任务推理（第五节E）有较大影响。

items.en: The results of the language following experiment, shown in Figure 11, show a similar trend as Figure 10 — excluding ME or/and CE data leads to a significant degradation in performance. What differs now is that removing web data (no WD) causes significantly worse performance on out-of-distribution (OOD) objects — we conjecture that training with web data, which contains very broad knowledge of physical objects, allows the model to understand and follow language commands involving unseen object categories.
items.zh: 语言遵循实验的结果如图11所示，显示出与图10类似的趋势——排除ME或/和CE数据导致性能显著下降。现在不同的是，移除网络数据（无WD）导致分布外（OOD）物体的性能显著变差——我们推测，使用包含非常广泛物理物体知识的网络数据进行训练，使模型能够理解和遵循涉及未见物体类别的语言指令。

full.sec: V-D How does π0.5\pi_{0.5} compare to other VLAs?

items.capEn: Figure 12: Comparing π0.5\pi_{0.5} with other models. Our full model significantly outperforms both π0\pi_{0} and π0​-FAST\pi_{0}\text{-FAST}+Flow in the mock home test environments.
items.capZh: 图12：比较π0.5与其他模型。我们的完整模型在模拟家庭测试环境中显著优于π0和π0-FAST+Flow。

items.en: We compare π0.5\pi_{0.5} to the original π0\pi_{0} VLA as well as an improved version of π0\pi_{0} which we denote as π0\pi_{0}-FAST+Flow. This version is trained via the joint diffusion and FAST action prediction formulation from Equation (1), but on action data only, without the HL or WD datasets. These models provide a strong point of comparison, since π0\pi_{0} has been demonstrated to perform strongly on complex and dexterous mobile manipulation tasks, and the enhancement in π0\pi_{0}-FAST+Flow brings it as close to π0.5\pi_{0.5} as possible. π0.5\pi_{0.5} builds on these models with a combination of co-training tasks. For a fair comparison, all models receive the same cross-embodiment robot training set and are trained for a comparable number of steps. The differences then are: (1) π0.5\pi_{0.5} additionally uses HL and WD data; (2) π0.5\pi_{0.5} uses a hybrid training procedure, with discrete tokenized training in the pre-training phase, and training with a flow matching action expert only in the post-training phase, while π0\pi_{0} always uses the action expert. π0\pi_{0}-FAST+Flow follows the hybrid training recipe but is trained only with data containing robot actions and thus cannot perform high-level inference. The results in Figure 12 show that π0.5\pi_{0.5} significantly outperforms both π0\pi_{0} and our enhanced version. This result holds even when we allow for longer training up to 300k training steps of π0\pi_{0}, confirming that as in Pertsch et al. [64] training with FAST tokens is more effective in terms of compute than pure diffusion based training.
items.zh: 我们将π0.5与原始π0 VLA以及π0的改进版本（记为π0-FAST+Flow）进行比较。该版本通过公式(1)中的联合扩散和FAST动作预测公式训练，但仅使用动作数据，没有HL或WD数据集。这些模型提供了强有力的比较点，因为π0已被证明在复杂和灵巧的移动操作任务上表现强劲，而π0-FAST+Flow的增强使其尽可能接近π0.5。π0.5在这些模型的基础上结合了协同训练任务。为了公平比较，所有模型接收相同的跨具身机器人训练集，并训练了可比较的步数。差异在于：(1) π0.5额外使用了HL和WD数据；(2) π0.5使用混合训练流程，在预训练阶段使用离散标记化训练，在后训练阶段仅使用流匹配动作专家训练，而π0始终使用动作专家。π0-FAST+Flow遵循混合训练配方，但仅使用包含机器人动作的数据训练，因此无法进行高层推理。图12中的结果表明，π0.5显著优于π0和我们的增强版本。即使允许π0进行长达300k训练步数的训练，这一结果仍然成立，证实了与Pertsch等人[64]一致，使用FAST标记训练在计算效率上优于纯扩散训练。

full.sec: V-E How important is high-level inference?
full.secZh: 五-E 高层推理的重要性？

items.en: Finally, we evaluate the importance of high-level inference, and compare the performance of several alternative high-level inference methods. The high-level inference mechanism in π0.5\pi_{0.5} takes in a high-level command (e.g., “clean the bedroom”) and outputs the subtask to complete (e.g., “pick up pillow”), which is then used as context for inferring the lower-level actions, analogously to chain of thought inference [82]. While π0.5\pi_{0.5} uses a unified architecture where the same model performs both high-level and low-level inference, we can also construct baseline methods that either forego the high-level inference process and feed the task prompt directly into the low-level system, as is common in standard VLA models [92, 8], or use another model for high-level inference to ablate the importance of different dataset components in terms of their impact on the high-level policy. We consider the following methods and ablations, all of which use the full π0.5\pi_{0.5} low-level inference process with different high-level policies:
items.zh: 最后，我们评估了高层推理的重要性，并比较了几种替代高层推理方法的性能。π0.5中的高层推理机制接收高层指令（例如，“清洁卧室”）并输出要完成的子任务（例如，“拿起枕头”），然后将其作为推断低层动作的上下文，类似于思维链推理[82]。虽然π0.5使用统一架构，同一模型同时执行高层和低层推理，但我们也可以构建基线方法，要么放弃高层推理过程，直接将任务提示输入低层系统（这在标准VLA模型中很常见[92, 8]），要么使用另一个模型进行高层推理，以消融不同数据集组件对高层策略的影响。我们考虑了以下方法和消融实验，所有这些方法都使用完整的π0.5低层推理过程，但采用不同的高层策略：

items.en: π0.5\pi_{0.5} model for high-level and low-level inference.
items.zh: π0.5模型用于高层和低层推理。

items.en: no WD: an ablation of π0.5\pi_{0.5} that excludes web data.
items.zh: 无WD：π0.5的消融实验，排除网络数据。

items.en: no VI: an ablation of π0.5\pi_{0.5} that excludes the verbal instruction (VI) data.
items.zh: 无VI：π0.5的消融实验，排除语言指令（VI）数据。

items.en: implicit HL: no high-level inference at runtime but includes high-level data in training, which may teach the model about subtasks implicitly.
items.zh: 隐式HL：运行时无高层推理，但训练中包含高层数据，这可能会隐式地教会模型关于子任务的知识。

items.en: no HL: no high-level inference, and no high-level data in training at all.
items.zh: 无HL：无高层推理，且训练中完全没有高层数据。

items.en: GPT-4: use GPT-4 as the high-level policy, evaluating the importance of training the high-level policy on robot data. To align the model with our domain, we prompt GPT-4 with a description of the task and a list of the most used labels to choose from.
items.zh: GPT-4：使用GPT-4作为高层策略，评估在机器人数据上训练高层策略的重要性。为了使模型与我们的领域对齐，我们向GPT-4提供任务描述和可供选择的最常用标签列表。

items.en: human HL: use an expert human as an “oracle” high-level policy, to provide an upper bound on performance.
items.zh: 人类HL：使用专家人类作为“神谕”高层策略，以提供性能上限。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Evaluation of the high-level inference process. While the full π0.5\pi_{0.5} model with high-level and low-level inference attains the best results, using only low-level inference (“implicit HL”) with the full π0.5\pi_{0.5} model also benefits from the inclusion of high-level subtask examples in training. In contrast, excluding verbal instructions (no VI) or web data (no WD) leads to a significant degradation in performance, and zero-shot prompting a large API-based model (GPT-4) performs worse.
items.capZh: 图13：高层推理过程的评估。虽然具有高层和低层推理的完整π0.5模型获得了最佳结果，但仅使用低层推理（“隐式HL”）的完整π0.5模型也受益于训练中包含高层子任务示例。相比之下，排除语言指令（无VI）或网络数据（无WD）会导致性能显著下降，而零样本提示大型基于API的模型（GPT-4）表现更差。

items.en: The results of these experiments are shown in Figure 13. The full π0.5\pi_{0.5} model performs the best, and outperforms even the human HL “oracle” baseline. Perhaps surprisingly, the second best model is the implicit HL ablation, which does not perform any high-level inference, but includes the full data mixture, i.e. also subtask prediction, in training. This strongly suggests the importance of the co-training recipe used by our model: while there is a benefit to explicitly infer high-level subtasks, a significant portion of that benefit is already obtained simply by including subtask prediction data in the training mixture. The no HL ablation, excluding HL task even in training, performs significantly worse. The results also show that the relatively small verbal instruction dataset, which only constitutes about 11% of the high-level mobile manipulation examples, is critical to strong performance as the no VI ablation is significantly weaker. The no WD ablation is also significantly worse, indicating that much of the benefit of web data (perhaps unsurprisingly) lies in improving the high-level policy. Finally, the zero-shot GPT-4 ablation attains the worst performance, indicating the importance of adapting VLMs with robot data. We provide a detailed breakdown of performance on each task in Appendix A-D, Figure 17.
items.zh: 这些实验的结果如图13所示。完整的π0.5模型表现最佳，甚至优于人类HL“神谕”基线。令人惊讶的是，第二好的模型是隐式HL消融实验，它不执行任何高层推理，但在训练中包含了完整的数据混合，即也包括子任务预测。这强烈表明我们的模型使用的联合训练配方的重要性：虽然显式推断高层子任务有好处，但仅通过在训练混合中包含子任务预测数据，就已经获得了该好处的很大一部分。无HL消融实验（即使在训练中也排除HL任务）表现显著更差。结果还表明，相对较小的语言指令数据集（仅占高层移动操作示例的约11%）对强性能至关重要，因为无VI消融实验明显更弱。无WD消融实验也明显更差，表明网络数据的大部分好处（也许不足为奇）在于改进高层策略。最后，零样本GPT-4消融实验获得了最差的性能，表明使用机器人数据调整VLM的重要性。我们在附录A-D的图17中提供了每个任务性能的详细分解。

items.en: We described π0.5\pi_{0.5}, a co-trained model that builds on the π0\pi_{0} VLA to integrate a variety of data sources and enable generalization to new environments. The π0.5\pi_{0.5} VLA can control mobile manipulators to perform tasks in homes that were never seen in the training data, cleaning kitchens and bedrooms, making beds, hanging towels, and performing other multi-stage and dexterous behaviors. π0.5\pi_{0.5} is trained on about 400 hours of mobile manipulation data, but includes a much larger amount of data from other robots, including non-mobile manipulators in diverse environments and data collected under laboratory conditions. It is also co-trained jointly with data from the web, as well as high-level prediction data for outputting language commands based on robot observations. The generalization capabilities of π0.5\pi_{0.5} demonstrate that this co-training recipe facilitates effective transfer, enabling highly generalizable control of a mobile manipulator with only a medium-sized mobile manipulation dataset.
items.zh: 我们描述了π0.5，一个联合训练模型，它建立在π0 VLA之上，整合了多种数据源，并实现了对新环境的泛化。π0.5 VLA可以控制移动操作器在训练数据中从未见过的家庭中执行任务，清洁厨房和卧室、铺床、挂毛巾以及执行其他多阶段和灵巧的行为。π0.5在大约400小时的移动操作数据上训练，但包含了来自其他机器人的大量数据，包括非移动操作器在多样化环境中的数据以及在实验室条件下收集的数据。它还与来自网络的数据以及基于机器人观测输出语言命令的高层预测数据联合训练。π0.5的泛化能力表明，这种联合训练配方促进了有效的迁移，使得仅使用中等规模的移动操作数据集就能实现高度泛化的移动操作器控制。

items.en: π0.5\pi_{0.5} is not without its limitations. While our VLA exhibits broad generalization, it still makes mistakes. Some environments present persistent challenges (e.g., unfamiliar handles on drawers, or cabinets that are physically hard for the robot to open), some behaviors present challenges with partial observability (e.g., the robot arm occluding a spill that should be wiped), and in some cases the high-level sub-task inference is easily distracted (e.g., closing and opening a drawer multiple times while putting away items). Addressing these challenges with better co-training, transfer, and larger datasets is a promising direction for future work. Other future work directions could address the technical constraints of our method. While π0.5\pi_{0.5} can perform a variety of behaviors to clean up kitchens and bedrooms, it processes relatively simple prompts. The complexity of the prompts that the model can accommodate is determined by the training data, and more complex preferences and instructions could be incorporated by producing more intricate and diverse annotations, either with human labelers or synthetically. The model also uses a relatively modest context, and incorporating richer context and memory could make the model significantly more capable in settings with more partial observability, such as tasks that require navigating between different rooms or remembering where objects are stored. More broadly, π0.5\pi_{0.5} explores a particular combination of heterogeneous data sources, but the specific sources of data can be explored even more broadly. For instance, the ability of our system to learn from verbal instructions provides a powerful new supervision modality, and future work could explore this and other ways that people can provide robots with additional contextual knowledge. We hope that our work will serve as a foundation for a new generation of VLAs that exhibit broad generalization to diverse real-world environments.
items.zh: π0.5并非没有局限性。虽然我们的VLA表现出广泛的泛化能力，但它仍然会犯错。一些环境存在持续挑战（例如，抽屉上不熟悉的手柄，或机器人难以物理打开的橱柜），一些行为在部分可观测性方面存在挑战（例如，机器人手臂遮挡了应该擦拭的溢出物），在某些情况下，高层子任务推理容易分心（例如，在收纳物品时多次关闭和打开抽屉）。通过更好的联合训练、迁移和更大的数据集来应对这些挑战是未来工作的一个有前景的方向。其他未来工作方向可以解决我们方法的技术限制。虽然π0.5可以执行各种行为来清洁厨房和卧室，但它处理相对简单的提示。模型能够适应的提示复杂性由训练数据决定，更复杂的偏好和指令可以通过生成更精细和多样化的注释来纳入，无论是通过人工标注员还是合成方式。该模型还使用了相对适中的上下文，纳入更丰富的上下文和记忆可以使模型在部分可观测性更强的设置中显著更有能力，例如需要在不同房间之间导航或记住物品存放位置的任务。更广泛地说，π0.5探索了异构数据源的特定组合，但可以更广泛地探索具体的数据源。例如，我们的系统从语言指令中学习的能力提供了一种强大的新监督模态，未来的工作可以探索这种以及人们可以为机器人提供额外上下文知识的其他方式。我们希望我们的工作能够成为新一代VLA的基础，这些VLA能够广泛泛化到多样化的真实世界环境。
